metadata.build_summary: Iteration 2 applies Judge iteration 1 verdict (iterate). Changes: (S001) IDS1->FEA4 activator added [E129]; (S002) FEA4->BA1 cross-module activation added [E016]; (S003) RA1->Axillary_Meristem(-) determinacy crosstalk added [E134]; (S004) UB2 restructured as multi-output SBP scaffold with direct edges to KN1/BIF2/ZMTCP30 [E066/E068/E070]; (S005) Cytokinin->ZmCKX homeostatic negative-feedback added [E119], ZMCKX now non-source; (S006) FEA4->RA3 direct transcriptional activation added [E017], RA3 now non-source; (S007) removed FEA2->IM_Size (N007) to resolve 9-input hub overload - FEA2 signal still reaches IM_Size via CT2 and ZmCRN complex partners. IM_Size now at 8 inputs. Coverage ratios improved: FEA4 0.25->0.58, IDS1 0.38->0.50, UB2 0.38->0.75.

nodes.description: CLE peptide ligand signaling through FEA2 and TD1 receptors to restrict inflorescence meristem size. Aliased in literature as ZmCLE7. Canonical CLV3-like ligand.

CLE14-like peptide ligand activating FEA2 and FEA3 receptors to repress ZmWUS1 in the rib zone. Parallel arm to ZmCLE7. Literature-gap: no curated maize upstream transcriptional regulator (per literature_judge G005); treated as biological source node.
Five redundant ZmEPFL peptide ligands bind the ZmER receptor kinase family to restrict IM size. Composite node (ZmEPFL1-5) reflecting redundancy reported by Liu 2026.
CC-type glutaredoxin (aka MSCA1) that post-translationally inhibits FEA4 activity; Abphyl2 dominant GOF enlarges meristem by blocking FEA4-mediated IM restriction.

CK-degrading oxidase/dehydrogenase family activated transcriptionally by CK signalling (homeostatic negative feedback). Provides the degradation arm of CK homeostasis alongside ZMIPT synthesis. Family composite reflecting ZmCKX1/3/5 redundancy.

Activating ARF that binds AuxRE motifs in BA1 regulatory region; ARF-driven BA1 transcription is gated by BIF1/BIF4 Aux/IAA repression until auxin-TIR1 degrades them. Modelled as source because no curated upstream transcriptional regulator resolved in current extraction.
LOB-domain TF acting upstream of RA1 in the ramosa branching-suppression pathway; ra2 ears show enhanced branching.
TPP trehalose-6-phosphate phosphatase dephosphorylating T6P; direct transcriptional target of FEA4 bZIP. Provides metabolic-signalling gate of the ramosa pathway feeding RA1 and T6P nodes.
TOPLESS-family co-repressor recruited by RA1 and other EAR-motif TFs to repress target genes; rel2 ears have increased KRN with ~16% enlarged IM.
tasselseed4 encodes miR172 which represses the AP2-like SPM regulators IDS1 and SID1 by mRNA cleavage.
Age-regulated miRNA targeting SBP-box mRNAs (UB2, UB3, TSH4). Literature-gap: no curated maize TF regulator (per literature_judge G015); source node.
WD40 repeat protein that interacts with DUF1644 to negatively regulate grain number; cloned KRN QTL under convergent selection in maize and rice.
5-phosphatase regulating phosphoinositol signaling; higher-expression allele increases ear weight 10-12% via increased KRN. Literature alias: KRN5b (lowercase b).

nodes.full_name: KERNEL NUMBER PER ROW 6 (Ser/Thr protein kinase)
nodes.description: Serine/threonine kinase phosphorylating AGAP; KNR6 OE increases kernels-per-row and KRN. Literature-gap: no curated upstream transcriptional regulator (per literature_judge G015); source node.
Low R:FR shade signal; End-of-day FR and WL+FR activate UB2 to accelerate SAM-to-IM transition.
LRR-RLP receptor for both ZmCLE7 and ZmFCP1 peptides; partners with CT2 (Galpha) and ZmCRN pseudokinase; fea2 enlarges meristem and increases KRN.
LRR-RLP receptor dedicated to ZmFCP1 peptide; restricts ZmWUS1 expression in the rib zone. Second arm of CLE-CLV Perception Gate.
LRR-receptor kinase, maize CLV1 ortholog, perceives CLV3-like CLE peptides including ZmCLE7; td1 ears are fasciated with extra rows.
Heterotrimeric G-alpha subunit that couples FEA2 to downstream meristem-restricting signals; ct2 has enlarged SAMs and fasciated ears.
Pseudokinase partnering with FEA2; Zmcrn mutants have larger vegetative shoot meristems. Component of FEA2 receptor complex.
bZIP TF that promotes peripheral differentiation and limits IM size; fea4 ears have enlarged SAM and increased KRN. Also directly activates RA1 to impose SPM determinacy.
ERECTA-family LRR-RLK activated by ZmEPFL peptides; restricts IM size via ZmWUS1. Zmer1 CRISPR yields enlarged IMs and increased KRN (Liu 2026).
Homeodomain TF, central organizing-center stem-cell maintainer of the IM; spatially integrates repression by FEA3/ZmER1/ABPH1 and activation by Type_B_ARR cytokinin signaling.
Class-I KNOX TF maintaining indeterminate meristem identity; directly activates auxin biosynthesis/transport/perception (VT2, SPI1, ZMPIN1A, TIR1) and cytokinin biosynthesis (ZMIPT).
Rate-limiting enzyme of cytokinin biosynthesis (composite over IPT/LOG-family CK-activating enzymes); KN1-regulated in the meristem.
Composite hormone node representing active CK forms that maintain IM organizing center. Biosynth-degrad balance: ZMIPT synthesizes, ZMCKX degrades, UB3 represses CK biosynthesis/signaling.

nodes.full_name: Type-B ARR (maize Type-B response regulator family)
nodes.description: Composite Type-B ARR TF activated by cytokinin (via AHK-AHP phosphorelay, collapsed here); directly binds AGATAT motifs in ZmWUS1 promoter and induces ABPH1 (feedback type-A ARR).
CK-inducible type-A response regulator providing negative feedback; dampens ZmWUS1 and promotes ZMPIN1A expression / auxin transport.
TAA1 co-ortholog that converts Trp to IPyA, the precursor of IAA biosynthesis; vt2 tassels lack branches/spikelets and ears have severe kernel reduction.
YUCCA-family flavin monooxygenase for local auxin biosynthesis; spi1 ears have fewer kernels and AM-initiation defects.
Indole-3-acetic acid, the primary auxin. Perception Gate: ligand for TIR1/AFB receptors. Biosynth from VT2+SPI1.
Composite F-box auxin coreceptor (TIR1/AFB family) forming SCF complex that ubiquitinates Aux/IAA repressors in response to auxin; KN1-regulated.

nodes.full_name: BARREN INFLORESCENCE 1 (Aux/IAA repressor; composite with BIF4)
nodes.description: Aux/IAA transcriptional repressor; composite representative of the BIF1/BIF4 functional pair that directly represses ARF-mediated BA1 transcription. Auxin-induced TIR1 degrades BIF1/BIF4.
PINOID-family kinase that phosphorylates ZMPIN1A to set polarity and co-localizes with BA1 in the nucleus to promote AM initiation. UB3-regulated.
Auxin efflux carrier establishing basipetal PAT; polarity set by BIF2 phosphorylation. Paralog ZmPIN1b not included separately (redundant).
bHLH TF required for axillary meristem initiation; activated by ARF6 and BIF2, repressed by BIF1 Aux/IAA. ba1 mutants lack all axillary meristems.

nodes.full_name: BARREN STALK2 (bHLH heterodimer partner of BA1)
nodes.description: bHLH co-ortholog of LAX2 that heterodimerizes with BA1 in the nucleus; required for reproductive axillary meristem development. ba2 mutants fail to produce ears.
C2H2 zinc-finger TF imposing SPM determinacy and suppressing indeterminate ear branching; integrates RA2, RA3, FEA4, and IDS1 inputs.
Sugar-signaling metabolite dephosphorylated by RA3 TPP activity. Elevated T6P (ra3 KO) promotes Ear_Branching by disrupting SPM determinacy.

nodes.full_name: INDETERMINATE SPIKELET 1 (AP2-domain TF; KRN1; Ts6 locus)
nodes.description: AP2-domain TF repressing indeterminate lateral branch meristem fates and promoting SPM-to-spikelet determinacy; Wang 2019 cloned this as the KRN1 major QTL. Aliases: KRN1, Ts6.
AP2-domain paralog of IDS1; redundantly controls SPM determinacy; ids1;sid1 doubles are the most severe extra-branching phenotype.
SBP-box TF that directly binds and activates UB3 via the KRN4 distal enhancer (Du 2020); shade-induced via FarRed_Light; miR156 target.

SBP-box TF restricting lateral-primordium initiation rate; cis-regulatory variation at its distal enhancer (known as KRN4) is the cloned KRN4 QTL. Alias: KRN4. Directly activates BIF2 and ZMTCP30; represses cytokinin biosynthesis/signaling via LOG1/Type-A ARR; maintains KN1 in central meristem.
SBP-box TF redundant with UB2/UB3 in restricting SPM-initiation rate; miR156 target.
TCP-family TF directly activated by UB3 (Kong 2022); Zmtcp30 has decreased tassel branch number, reduced grain number and abnormal ear development.
ARF-GAP phosphorylated by KNR6; AGAP CRISPR produces shorter IM and fewer kernels per row.

Quantitative size of the ear inflorescence meristem. Integrator process aggregating CLV-CLE restriction (FEA2/TD1/CT2/ZMCRN/FEA4), ERECTA-EPFL restriction (via ZmWUS1), organizing-center maintenance (ZmWUS1/KN1), AGAP kinase signaling, and SBP/TOPLESS repression (UB3/REL2). Directly scales with the number of SPM tiers and hence KRN.
Process of AM formation at IM flanks driven by BA1/BA2 bHLH heterodimer and local auxin maxima (ZMPIN1A transport); prerequisite to SPM formation.

Aberrant conversion of SPMs to indeterminate branches. Suppressed by RA1/REL2/IDS1/SID1 SPM-determinacy module; promoted by elevated T6P (ra3 KO). Negatively affects KRN by disrupting ordered row formation.

Final trait: number of kernel rows per ear. Proximal output of IM size (determining number of SPMs per tier), axillary-meristem density, ZMTCP30 and KRN5b QTL modifiers, with negative contributions from ear branching, KRN2 WD40 repression, and TSH4 SBP peripheral restriction.

edges.mechanism: CLE peptide ligand binds LRR-RLP receptor FEA2

evidence.title: The CLAVATA receptor FASCIATED EAR2 responds to distinct CLE peptides by signaling through two downstream effectors
evidence.authors: Je BI, Xu F, Wu Q, Liu L, Meeley R, Gallagher JP, Corcilius L, Payne RJ, Bartlett ME, Jackson D
evidence.evidence_sentence: FEA2 transmits signaling from two distinct CLE peptides, the maize CLV3 ortholog ZmCLE7 and ZmFCP1
evidence.claim: ZmCLE7 peptide signals through FEA2 receptor

edges.mechanism: CLV3-like peptide perceived by TD1 (CLV1 ortholog) LRR receptor kinase

evidence.title: thick tassel dwarf1 encodes a putative maize ortholog of the Arabidopsis CLAVATA1 leucine-rich repeat receptor-like kinase
evidence.authors: Bommert P, Lunde C, Nardmann J, Vollbrecht E, Running M, Jackson D, Hake S, Werr W
evidence.evidence_sentence: TD1 functions as maize CLV1 ortholog perceiving CLV3-like peptides; td1 fasciated ears phenocopy CLV-pathway loss
evidence.claim: ZmCLE7 signal requires TD1 receptor kinase

edges.mechanism: ZmFCP1 also signals through FEA2 (shared receptor with ZmCLE7)

evidence.title: The CLAVATA receptor FASCIATED EAR2 responds to distinct CLE peptides by signaling through two downstream effectors
evidence.authors: Je BI, Xu F, Wu Q, Liu L, Meeley R, Gallagher JP, Corcilius L, Payne RJ, Bartlett ME, Jackson D
evidence.evidence_sentence: FEA2 also participates in ZmFCP1 signaling, in addition to controlling responses to the maize CLV3 ortholog

edges.mechanism: ZmFCP1 peptide ligand binds FEA3 LRR-RLP receptor in the rib zone (parallel arm)

evidence.title: The CLAVATA receptor FASCIATED EAR2 responds to distinct CLE peptides by signaling through two downstream effectors
evidence.authors: Je BI, Xu F, Wu Q, Liu L, Meeley R, Gallagher JP, Corcilius L, Payne RJ, Bartlett ME, Jackson D
evidence.evidence_sentence: Both fea3 and fea2 mutants are resistant to the ZmFCP1 peptide

edges.mechanism: FEA2 receptor couples to heterotrimeric G-alpha subunit CT2 in complex

evidence.title: The CLAVATA receptor FASCIATED EAR2 responds to distinct CLE peptides by signaling through two downstream effectors
evidence.authors: Je BI, Xu F, Wu Q, Liu L, Meeley R, Gallagher JP, Corcilius L, Payne RJ, Bartlett ME, Jackson D
evidence.evidence_sentence: FEA2 interacted with CT2 in co-immunoprecipitation (co-IP) experiments

edges.mechanism: FEA2 receptor partners with ZmCRN pseudokinase in the signaling complex

evidence.title: The CLAVATA receptor FASCIATED EAR2 responds to distinct CLE peptides by signaling through two downstream effectors
evidence.authors: Je BI, Xu F, Wu Q, Liu L, Meeley R, Gallagher JP, Corcilius L, Payne RJ, Bartlett ME, Jackson D
evidence.evidence_sentence: ZmCRN-mCherry was able to pull down FEA2-Myc

edges.mechanism: TD1 LRR-RLK relays CLV3-like CLE signal to restrict meristem proliferation

evidence.title: thick tassel dwarf1 encodes a putative maize ortholog of the Arabidopsis CLAVATA1 leucine-rich repeat receptor-like kinase
evidence.authors: Bommert P, Lunde C, Nardmann J, Vollbrecht E, Running M, Jackson D, Hake S, Werr W
evidence.evidence_sentence: td1 female inflorescence is fasciated, with extra rows of kernels

edges.mechanism: G-alpha CT2 relays CLV signal from FEA2 complex to restrict stem-cell proliferation

evidence.title: The CLAVATA receptor FASCIATED EAR2 responds to distinct CLE peptides by signaling through two downstream effectors
evidence.authors: Je BI, Xu F, Wu Q, Liu L, Meeley R, Gallagher JP, Corcilius L, Payne RJ, Bartlett ME, Jackson D
evidence.evidence_sentence: ct2 mutants had larger SAMs (157.1 +/- 11.8 micrometer)

edges.mechanism: ZmCRN pseudokinase in FEA2 complex restricts meristem proliferation

evidence.title: The CLAVATA receptor FASCIATED EAR2 responds to distinct CLE peptides by signaling through two downstream effectors
evidence.authors: Je BI, Xu F, Wu Q, Liu L, Meeley R, Gallagher JP, Corcilius L, Payne RJ, Bartlett ME, Jackson D
evidence.evidence_sentence: Zmcrn mutants had larger vegetative shoot meristems (130.0 +/- 4.1 micrometer, compared to 109.2 +/- 4.6 micrometer for normal sibs)

edges.mechanism: FEA3-ZmFCP1 signaling directly represses ZmWUS1 expression in the rib zone (canonical CLE-WUS repression)

evidence.title: Structural variation at the maize WUSCHEL1 locus alters stem cell organization in inflorescences
evidence.authors: Chen Z, Li W, Gaines C, Buck A, Galli M, Gallavotti A
evidence.evidence_sentence: A distinct ZmFCP1-FEA3 ligand-receptor combination prevents WUS gene expression in the rib zone

edges.mechanism: bZIP TF FEA4 promotes peripheral differentiation and limits meristem

evidence.title: FASCIATED EAR4 Encodes a bZIP Transcription Factor That Regulates Shoot Meristem Size in Maize
evidence.authors: Pautler M, Eveland AL, LaRue T, Yang F, Weeks R, Lunde C, Je BI, Meeley R, Komatsu M, Vollbrecht E, Sakai H, Jackson D
evidence.evidence_sentence: fea4 mutants were also characterized by a larger than normal SAM diameter

edges.mechanism: MSCA1/ABPH2 glutaredoxin post-translationally inhibits FEA4 activity (dominant Abphyl2 allele derepresses meristem)

evidence.title: A Maize Glutaredoxin Gene, Abphyl2, Regulates Shoot Meristem Size and Phyllotaxy
evidence.authors: Yang F, Bui HT, Pautler M, Llaca V, Johnston R, Lee BH, Kolbe A, Sakai H, Jackson D
evidence.evidence_sentence: FEA4 activity is negatively regulated by MSCA1 based on opposing meristem phenotypes and epistasis analysis

edges.mechanism: Five ZmEPFL peptide ligands bind and activate ZmER1 LRR-RLK (redundant composite ligand)

evidence.title: ERECTA genes and their ligands regulate shoot and inflorescence architecture in maize
evidence.authors: Liu X, Wang J, Li J, Kang L, Wang M, Huang Z, Man J, Huang X, Zhang Z, Yang F, Bartlett M, Wu L, Dong Z, Jackson D, Xu F
evidence.evidence_sentence: ZmER1 specifically binds to five EPFL peptides, which act redundantly in ear development regulation
evidence.claim: ZmEPFL peptides bind and activate ZmER1 receptor kinase

edges.mechanism: ZmER-EPFL signaling restricts ZmWUS1 activity; Zmwus1 partially suppresses Zmer1 enlarged-IM phenotype

evidence.title: ERECTA genes and their ligands regulate shoot and inflorescence architecture in maize
evidence.authors: Liu X, Wang J, Li J, Kang L, Wang M, Huang Z, Man J, Huang X, Zhang Z, Yang F, Bartlett M, Wu L, Dong Z, Jackson D, Xu F
evidence.evidence_sentence: ZmWUS1 mediates the pathway's effects; mutation in Zmwus1 partially suppresses the enlarged IM of Zmer1 mutants
evidence.claim: ZmER signaling acts through ZmWUS1 to restrict meristem proliferation

edges.mechanism: Cytokinin signaling activates Type-B ARRs (AHK-AHP phosphorelay collapsed into this single gate)

evidence.title: Genetic dissection of cis-regulatory control of ZmWUSCHEL1 expression by type B RESPONSE REGULATORS
evidence.authors: Chen Z, Cortes L, Gallavotti A
evidence.evidence_sentence: Cytokinin signaling activates type-B ARRs, which then regulate ZmWUS1

edges.mechanism: Type-B ARRs directly bind AGATAT motifs in the ZmWUS1 proximal promoter and activate transcription

evidence.title: Structural variation at the maize WUSCHEL1 locus alters stem cell organization in inflorescences
evidence.authors: Chen Z, Li W, Gaines C, Buck A, Galli M, Gallavotti A
evidence.evidence_sentence: type-B ARRs directly bind to the WUS proximal promoter region and activate its expression

edges.mechanism: Type-B ARRs induce CK-responsive type-A response regulator ABPH1 (self-limiting feedback)

evidence.title: Control of phyllotaxy by the cytokinin-inducible response regulator homologue ABPHYL1
evidence.authors: Giulini A, Wang J, Jackson D
evidence.evidence_sentence: ABPH1 transcription is CK-inducible, downstream of type-B ARRs

edges.mechanism: Type-A ARR ABPH1 provides negative feedback by damping CK -> ZmWUS1 activation (closes the canonical CK/WUS loop)

evidence.title: Structural variation at the maize WUSCHEL1 locus alters stem cell organization in inflorescences
evidence.authors: Chen Z, Li W, Gaines C, Buck A, Galli M, Gallavotti A
evidence.evidence_sentence: Type-A ARR feedback negatively modulates CK->WUS activation

edges.mechanism: ZmWUS1 homeoprotein is transported via plasmodesmata to the apical domain to maintain the stem-cell pool

evidence.title: Structural variation at the maize WUSCHEL1 locus alters stem cell organization in inflorescences
evidence.authors: Chen Z, Li W, Gaines C, Buck A, Galli M, Gallavotti A
evidence.evidence_sentence: WUS is transported via plasmodesmata into the apical domain to promote proliferation of stem cells

edges.mechanism: ZmIPT catalyzes rate-limiting CK biosynthesis (composite with LOG-family CK-activating enzymes)

evidence.title: Hormonal influence on maize inflorescence development and reproduction
evidence.authors: Chaudhry A, Chen Z, Gallavotti A
evidence.evidence_sentence: IPT is the rate-limiting enzyme for cytokinin biosynthesis

edges.mechanism: ZmCKX cytokinin oxidase irreversibly degrades cytokinins (degradation arm of CK homeostasis)

evidence.title: Hormonal influence on maize inflorescence development and reproduction
evidence.authors: Chaudhry A, Chen Z, Gallavotti A
evidence.evidence_sentence: Cytokinin oxidases catalyse irreversible degradation of cytokinins

edges.mechanism: UB3 directly binds LOG1 and Type-A ARR promoters, repressing CK biosynthesis and signaling (net CK down when UB3 up)

evidence.title: UNBRANCHED3 regulates branching by modulating cytokinin biosynthesis and signaling in maize and rice
evidence.authors: Du Y, Liu L, Li M, Fang S, Shen X, Chu J, Zhang Z
evidence.evidence_sentence: UB3 could bind and regulate the promoters of LONELY GUY1 (LOG1) and Type-A response regulators (ARRs), which participate in cytokinin biosynthesis and signaling; in maize mutants lacking functional UB3 cytokinin biosynthesis-related genes were up-regulated and degradation-related genes were down-regulated
evidence.claim: UB3 represses cytokinin biosynthesis and signaling

edges.mechanism: VT2 tryptophan aminotransferase (TAA1 co-ortholog) converts Trp to IPyA in IAA biosynthesis

evidence.title: vanishing tassel2 encodes a grass-specific tryptophan aminotransferase required for vegetative and reproductive development in maize
evidence.authors: Phillips KA, Skirpan AL, Liu X, Christensen A, Slewinski TL, Hudson C, Barazesh S, Cohen JD, Malcomber S, McSteen P
evidence.evidence_sentence: vt2 encodes a co-ortholog of TAA1 which converts Trp to indole-3-pyruvic acid

edges.mechanism: SPI1 YUCCA-like flavin monooxygenase converts IPyA to IAA (local auxin biosynthesis)

evidence.title: sparse inflorescence1 encodes a monocot-specific YUCCA-like gene required for vegetative and reproductive development in maize
evidence.authors: Gallavotti A, Barazesh S, Malcomber S, Hall D, Jackson D, Schmidt RJ, McSteen P
evidence.evidence_sentence: spi1 encodes a flavin monooxygenase similar to the YUCCA genes involved in local auxin biosynthesis

edges.mechanism: Auxin is the TIR1/AFB F-box coreceptor ligand (Perception Gate; Auxin's only outgoing edge)

evidence.title: Auxin signaling modules regulate maize inflorescence architecture
evidence.authors: Galli M, Liu Q, Moss BL, Malcomber S, Li W, Gaines C, Federici S, Roshkovan J, Meeley R, Nemhauser JL, Gallavotti A
evidence.evidence_sentence: BIF1/BIF4 are auxin-responsive Aux/IAAs; TIR1-dependent degradation removes repression

edges.mechanism: TIR1 SCF complex ubiquitinates BIF1 (and BIF4) Aux/IAA repressors for proteasomal degradation

evidence.title: Auxin signaling modules regulate maize inflorescence architecture
evidence.authors: Galli M, Liu Q, Moss BL, Malcomber S, Li W, Gaines C, Federici S, Roshkovan J, Meeley R, Nemhauser JL, Gallavotti A
evidence.evidence_sentence: TIR1-dependent degradation of BIF1/BIF4 Aux/IAAs removes repression

edges.mechanism: Aux/IAA BIF1/BIF4 directly represses ARF-driven BA1 transcription until auxin-induced degradation removes them

evidence.title: Auxin signaling modules regulate maize inflorescence architecture
evidence.authors: Galli M, Liu Q, Moss BL, Malcomber S, Li W, Gaines C, Federici S, Roshkovan J, Meeley R, Nemhauser JL, Gallavotti A
evidence.evidence_sentence: BIF1 and BIF4 are integral parts of functionally redundant signaling modules that directly control the transcription of BA1; double mutant shows no BA1 expression

edges.mechanism: ARF TFs bind AuxRE motifs in BA1 regulatory region and activate transcription when released from BIF1/BIF4

evidence.title: Auxin signaling modules regulate maize inflorescence architecture
evidence.authors: Galli M, Liu Q, Moss BL, Malcomber S, Li W, Gaines C, Federici S, Roshkovan J, Meeley R, Nemhauser JL, Gallavotti A
evidence.evidence_sentence: ARF4, ARF16, ARF27, ARF29, and ARF34 strongly bound to all four probes of BA1 regulatory region

edges.mechanism: BIF2 PINOID-like kinase phosphorylates ZmPIN1a, regulating efflux carrier polarity in vivo

evidence.title: ZmPIN1a and ZmPIN1b Encode Two Novel Putative Candidates for Polar Auxin Transport
evidence.authors: Carraro N, Forestan C, Canova S, Traas J, Varotto S
evidence.evidence_sentence: BIF2 phosphorylates ZmPIN1a and regulates ZmPIN1a subcellular localization in vivo

edges.mechanism: BIF2 kinase and BA1 co-localize in the nucleus; BIF2 functions by direct interaction with BA1 to promote AM initiation

evidence.title: The Relationship between Auxin Transport and Maize Branching
evidence.authors: Gallavotti A, Yang Y, Schmidt RJ, Jackson D
evidence.evidence_sentence: BIF2 and BA1 co-localize in the nucleus; BIF2 functions by interacting with BA1 to promote axillary meristem initiation

edges.mechanism: Basipetal PAT by ZmPIN1a establishes auxin maxima at IM flanks that position axillary meristems; NPA inhibition abolishes AM formation

evidence.title: The Relationship between Auxin Transport and Maize Branching
evidence.authors: Gallavotti A, Yang Y, Schmidt RJ, Jackson D
evidence.evidence_sentence: NPA treatment (PAT inhibition) completely abolished ability of IM to form any axillary meristem
evidence.claim: Polar auxin transport (via ZmPIN1a) required for AM initiation

edges.mechanism: bHLH TF BA1 is required cell-autonomously for AM initiation; ba1 mutants completely lack axillary meristems

evidence.title: The Relationship between Auxin Transport and Maize Branching
evidence.authors: Gallavotti A, Yang Y, Schmidt RJ, Jackson D
evidence.evidence_sentence: ba1 mutants completely lack all axillary meristems, resulting in a sterile plant

edges.mechanism: BA1 and BA2 cooperate as bHLH heterodimer partners; BA1 dosage affects ba2 phenotype indicating same-pathway action

evidence.title: The barren stalk2 Gene Is Required for Axillary Meristem Development in Maize
evidence.authors: Yao H, Skirpan A, Wardell B, Matthes MS, Best NB, McCubbin T, Durbak A, Smith T, Malcomber S, McSteen P
evidence.evidence_sentence: ba1 shows a gene dosage effect in ba2 mutants, providing further evidence that BA1 and BA2 act together in the same pathway
evidence.claim: BA1 acts together with BA2 in the same pathway for AM formation

edges.mechanism: BA2 bHLH protein heterodimerizes with BA1 and is required for AM initiation; ba2 fails to produce ears

evidence.title: The barren stalk2 Gene Is Required for Axillary Meristem Development in Maize
evidence.authors: Yao H, Skirpan A, Wardell B, Matthes MS, Best NB, McCubbin T, Durbak A, Smith T, Malcomber S, McSteen P
evidence.evidence_sentence: The ba2 gene encodes a protein that co-localizes and heterodimerizes with BA1 in the nucleus; ba2 mutant fails to produce ears and has fewer branches and spikelets in the tassel
evidence.claim: BA2 is required for AM initiation and reproductive development

edges.mechanism: ABPH1 positively regulates auxin levels and PIN1 signaling (CK-auxin crosstalk at IM periphery)

evidence.title: Studies of aberrant phyllotaxy1 Mutants of Maize Indicate Complex Interactions between Auxin and Cytokinin Signaling
evidence.authors: Lee BH, Johnston R, Yang Y, Gallavotti A, Kojima M, Travencolo BA, Costa LF, Sakakibara H, Jackson D
evidence.evidence_sentence: ABPH1 acts both as a positive regulator of auxin levels and PIN1 signaling

edges.mechanism: KN1 binds and upregulates tryptophan aminotransferase VT2 (auxin biosynthesis arm of the KNOX regulatory network)

evidence.title: Unraveling the KNOTTED1 regulatory network in maize meristems
evidence.authors: Bolduc N, Yilmaz A, Mejia-Guerra MK, Morohashi K, O'Connor D, Grotewold E, Hake S
evidence.evidence_sentence: vt2 tryptophan aminotransferase shows significant up-regulation in Kn1-N leaves

edges.mechanism: KN1 ChIP-seq peaks near YUCCA-family auxin biosynthesis loci including SPI1, with up-regulation in Kn1-N

evidence.title: Unraveling the KNOTTED1 regulatory network in maize meristems
evidence.authors: Bolduc N, Yilmaz A, Mejia-Guerra MK, Morohashi K, O'Connor D, Grotewold E, Hake S
evidence.evidence_sentence: KN1 binds near genes coding for enzymes that synthesize auxin and maintain its homeostasis; KN1-bound genes involved in the synthesis of auxin and its precursor tryptophan are also up-regulated
evidence.claim: KN1 directly binds and positively regulates auxin biosynthesis enzymes including SPI1

edges.mechanism: KN1 ChIP-seq peaks near PIN efflux transporter loci; Kn1-N GOF up-regulates PIN family (including ZmPIN1a)

evidence.title: Unraveling the KNOTTED1 regulatory network in maize meristems
evidence.authors: Bolduc N, Yilmaz A, Mejia-Guerra MK, Morohashi K, O'Connor D, Grotewold E, Hake S
evidence.evidence_sentence: auxin efflux transporters of the PIN and ABCB families up-regulated in Kn1-N leaves, with ChIP-seq peaks identified near these loci
evidence.claim: KN1 directly up-regulates PIN auxin efflux transporters

edges.mechanism: KN1 positively regulates CK biosynthesis-related genes including the rice LONELY GUY ortholog (CK-activating enzyme, included in ZMIPT composite)

evidence.title: Unraveling the KNOTTED1 regulatory network in maize meristems
evidence.authors: Bolduc N, Yilmaz A, Mejia-Guerra MK, Morohashi K, O'Connor D, Grotewold E, Hake S
evidence.evidence_sentence: KN1 likely positively regulates a gene similar to the rice CK synthesis gene LONELY GUY
evidence.claim: KN1 positively regulates cytokinin biosynthesis (LOG/IPT family)

edges.mechanism: KN1 ChIP-seq peaks near AFB-like F-box auxin coreceptor genes (TIR1/AFB family)

evidence.title: Unraveling the KNOTTED1 regulatory network in maize meristems
evidence.authors: Bolduc N, Yilmaz A, Mejia-Guerra MK, Morohashi K, O'Connor D, Grotewold E, Hake S
evidence.evidence_sentence: KN1 binds near genes coding for AFB-like genes that are characterized as F-box auxin coreceptors in Arabidopsis
evidence.claim: KN1 binds TIR1/AFB auxin coreceptor loci

evidence.claim: FEA4 restricts kernel row number directly in addition to its inhibition of meristem size
